name: Kadena Air Base
tagline: The 'Keystone of the Pacific': the Air Force's largest combat wing, on Okinawa.
_note: OCONUS (Okinawa, Japan). US-only feeds intentionally omitted (overseas uses OHA + COLA, not BAH; no RentCast or SchoolDigger). Weather via the Open-Meteo global fallback. Facts verified from official sources (kadena.af.mil, dodea.edu/pacific, TRICARE Overseas, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Host wing: 18th Wing (the Air Force's largest combat wing)
data.Command: Fifth Air Force / Pacific Air Forces
data.Region: OCONUS (overseas tour; OHA and COLA, not BAH)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Kadena Air Base on Okinawa is known as the 'Keystone of the Pacific.' Its host 18th Wing is the largest combat wing in the Air Force, and the base is a central node for U.S. airpower in the Indo-Pacific.
Okinawa hosts a large, multi-service U.S. presence, and Kadena is the hub of it, with nearly 18,000 Americans in the Team Kadena community.

sections.title: Island Life on Okinawa: On Base or Off

data.subtitle: Okinawa is a beautiful, dense subtropical island with a strong American community. Command sponsorship and the on-base versus off-base choice define the tour, and OHA plus COLA help with island costs.
intro: As with any overseas tour, command sponsorship is what brings your family at government expense and unlocks housing, DoDEA schools, and benefits. Confirm it early through your gaining command.

Housing splits between on-base and off-base rentals on the Japanese economy. On-base is convenient and removes the language barrier from daily life; off-base offers a fuller Okinawan experience, with OHA covering rent up to a ceiling. The island is compact, so beaches and dive sites are never far, but expect typhoon season and dense traffic.

recommendations.advice: Command sponsorship determines whether your family comes and gets housing, schools, and benefits; sort it through your unit early.
On-base housing is convenient to work, schools, and amenities and is in high demand; get on the list early.
Renting on the local economy offers a fuller island experience; OHA offsets rent and utilities up to a ceiling, and COLA helps with everyday costs.
Okinawa's beaches, diving, and culture are a highlight; plan around typhoon season and the island's heavy traffic.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access and in-processing are handled through the installation, and overseas access rules differ from stateside bases. You will need status documents under the U.S.-Japan SOFA.
Confirm the current visitor and access requirements on the official Kadena site before you arrive.

sections.title: Housing, OHA & COLA
data: Families choose between on-base housing and renting on the Okinawan economy. On-base housing is convenient and in high demand, so get on the list early.

Overseas, the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) replaces stateside BAH for off-base rentals, individualized by rank, dependents, and actual rent up to a ceiling. An overseas cost-of-living allowance (COLA) also helps with island costs. The Housing Office assigns on-base housing and helps with off-base rentals.

sections.title: Schools
data: Children attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools rather than local districts. DoDEA Pacific operates several elementary, middle, and high schools serving Kadena and the surrounding Okinawa installations.
Start DoDEA registration early and confirm the specific schools, boundaries, and required records on the DoDEA site as you plan your move.

data.Care: Hospital and clinic care on base; some specialty care referred to other military or host-nation facilities

data.Exchange: Kadena Exchange (AAFES / BX), one of the largest overseas

intro: An overseas PCS has extra steps beyond a stateside move. Start these early, ideally before you leave.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: An accompanied (command-sponsored) tour is what lets your family come at government expense and access housing, schools, and the commissary. Confirm it through your gaining command.

steps.title: Get no-fee passports and SOFA documents
steps.text: Apply for no-fee passports for family members and the U.S.-Japan SOFA documents that let you live in-country. Tourist passports are separate.

steps.title: Line up lodging and a sponsor
steps.text: Arrange on-base temporary lodging and connect with your unit sponsor before you fly. You can claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA).

steps.title: Plan household goods and your vehicle
steps.text: Overseas shipments go by sea with weight limits. Many families buy a local vehicle rather than ship one; confirm the rules and inspections.

steps.title: Register the kids with DoDEA
steps.text: Children attend DoDEA Pacific schools; start registration early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up TRICARE Overseas
steps.text: Enroll in TRICARE Overseas and get a primary care manager at the 18th Medical Group.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Kadena?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive OHA instead of BAH for off-base rentals, plus an overseas cost-of-living allowance (COLA). On-base housing is government-provided.
Both are common. On-base is convenient and removes the language barrier from daily life; off-base offers a fuller Okinawan experience with OHA covering the cost.
Subtropical and beautiful, with beaches and diving close by, but the island is dense with heavy traffic and a real typhoon season from summer into fall.
Often yes, but Japan has strict import and quarantine requirements and on-base pet policies. Start the process months early.
